mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations. A sustained downward trend in FEDFUNDS over the preceding 3 months signals monetary easing that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.54% suggests accommodative stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, which compresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates through demand destruction and margin compression.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.5 percentage points over preceding 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE at 4.7% provides baseline for deterioration detection
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth reflects consumer spending momentum and aggregate demand strength. Rising RSAFS signals robust economic activity that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:current RSAFS at 469,092M indicates baseline demand level
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive RSAFS growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth indicates expanding productive capacity and supply-side strength. Rising INDPRO combined with stable or declining inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals healthy real output growth without cost-push pressures, supporting profit margins and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.2% AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth < 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:current INDPRO at 103.86 and CPIAUCSL at 242.35
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods meeting both trigger conditions are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction effect (INDPRO × CPIAUCSL slope) is not statistically significant at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) lead broader economic activity by 6-12 months. Declining housing construction signals weakening forward demand expectations and reduced capital investment, which produces negative S&P 500 returns as the market reprices growth downward over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 10% over preceding 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances where HOUST declines > 10%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) drives consumer income and spending capacity. Rising employment combined with stable inflation produces real income growth that supports equity valuations. The mechanism requires both employment expansion and monetary accommodation to generate positive returns.
evidence_refs: fact:current PAYEMS at 145,303K and FEDFUNDS at 0.54%
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods meeting both trigger conditions are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL above 3% combined with rising federal funds rate signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations. This regime-specific mechanism produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when tightening is active.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp over preceding month AND 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation > 3.0%
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.54% and CPIAUCSL at 242.35
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction effect between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL is not statistically significant at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) represents aggregate economic output and future earnings capacity. Accelerating real GDP growth signals expanding corporate profit opportunities and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices earnings expectations upward.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
